summary: The `pnpm happytg update` implementation, local update/uninstall prompt package, synchronized documentation, and 0.4.9 release metadata are ready. Runtime `pnpm happytg verify` is environment-blocked in the isolated worktree by missing .env/Telegram token and local port conflicts, while release-check, lint, typecheck, test, and build passed.

checks.name: update-cli
checks.detail: `pnpm happytg update` delegates to the installer engine with safe current-checkout update defaults.

checks.name: prompt
checks.detail: Reusable prompt artifact exists and includes 10-role critique plus update/uninstall/release gates.

checks.name: docs
checks.detail: README, quickstart, installation, bootstrap, troubleshooting, self-hosting, runbook, release-process, changelog, and release notes were updated.

checks.name: release-metadata
checks.detail: Workspace package versions, CHANGELOG.md, and docs/releases/0.4.9.md are aligned; release check passed.

checks.name: repo-checks
checks.detail: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile, lint, typecheck, test, build, and task validation passed.

checks.name: happytg-verify
checks.detail: Environment-blocked in isolated worktree: .env and TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN are absent; ports 3001/80/443/3000 are occupied.

residualRisks: `pnpm happytg update` uses the installer repo-sync semantics, so dirty worktrees still require commit, stash, keep, or cancel decisions according to existing installer rules.
Environment-specific `pnpm happytg verify` still requires real `.env`, Telegram configuration, and available ports.
